content.problemStatement:
You are given a string numsnumsnums consisting of digits representing a large integer. Your task is to find the largest-valued **odd integer** (as a substring of numsnumsnums) that can be obtained.

A **substring** is a contiguous sequence of characters within the string.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindLargestOddSubstringfindLargestOddSubstringfindLargestOddSubstring – This function finds the largest-valued odd integer that can be obtained as a substring of the given numeric string.

Parameters
numnumnum : A string representing a large integer, consisting only of digits (0–90–90–9).
Return Value

This function **prints**:



The largest odd integer substring if it exists.
−1-1−1 if no odd integer substring can be formed.


Constraints
1≤∣num∣≤1051 \le |num| \le 10^51≤∣num∣≤105
The string numnumnum contains only digits 0–90–90–9
The string does **not** contain leading zeros
There is no limit on the size of the integer represented by the substring

Input Format

The first line contains a single string `num`.


Output Format


Print a single line:



The largest odd integer substring of `num`
Or `-1` if no odd substring exists
constraints: The sum of |num| over all test cases does not exceed 100000
num consists only of characters '0'–'9' and has no leading zeros
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single string num representing a non‑negative integer without leading zeros. The length of each num satisfies 1 ≤ |num| ≤ 100000.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a separate line the largest odd integer that can be obtained as a contiguous substring of num. If no odd digit exists in num, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: the rightmost odd digit is '7' at index 1, so the prefix "7" is the largest odd substring.\nTest case 2: no odd digit exists, answer is -1.\nTest case 3: the last digit '1' is odd, therefore the whole string is odd and maximal.

hidden.label: general case 2 – cut at last odd digit

solution.approach: The value of a numeric substring grows with its length when the leftmost digits are fixed. Therefore, the largest odd integer obtainable is the longest prefix that ends with an odd digit. Scan the string from the rightmost character towards the left until an odd digit (1,3,5,7,9) is found. If such a digit exists at position i, the answer is the prefix num[0..i]. If no odd digit exists, answer is -1. This scan is O(|num|) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) ≤ O(100000)
